title: lappago
intro: Lappago, also known as Euphorbia lathyris, is a prickly, annual herb that belongs to the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to induce vomiting and purging, making it effective in treating conditions such as poisoning and indigestion. The health benefits of lappago include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Lappago is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion in herbal medicine, and its seeds are also used in traditional remedies.
